title: Elaphrium stenophyllum
taxonomy: Elaphrium stenophyllum is a species within the family Fabaceae. It is also known as a part of the genus Elaphrium. The classification of Elaphrium stenophyllum places it in the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, and class Magnoliopsida. Further classification also includes the order Fabales and family Fabaceae. This complex classification system helps in understanding the plant's evolutionary relationships.
uses: Elaphrium stenophyllum is a plant species native to the Americas, however information on its specific medicinal uses is limited. It may possess antibacterial or anti-inflammatory properties, but concrete evidence is scarce. Elaphrium stenophyllum has been traditionally used by some indigenous communities to treat various ailments, although these uses are often anecdotal and not scientifically verified. More research is necessary to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal plant.
